data.jd_text: TENTEN Partners is retained on a confidential mandate to hire a Head of Engineering for a regulated fintech infrastructure business operating at the intersection of payments, settlement, and digital-asset enabled workflows. What You’ll Be Accountable For 1) Engineering Leadership & Operating System Build and lead a high-performance team across backend, frontend, platform/DevOps, and QA Set the engineering “operating improvements Architect for uptime and scale across transaction-heavy flows (payments + settlement) 4) Security-First Engineering (Regulated Standard) Embed secure SDLC: code review discipline, dependency scanning, secrets management, pen testing Harden APIs and integrations (authN/authZ, idempotency, encryption, least privilege) Ensure systems are audit-ready with defensible controls and clear documentation Partner with compliance/risk to meet regulatory and institutional partner expectations 5) Payments + Digital Asset Connectivity Oversee systems that touch transaction processing, settlement workflows, reconciliation, and audit trails Lead (or intelligently govern) stablecoin / blockchain connectivity, wallet operations, and monitoring where relevant Ensure operational resiliency: observability, reconciliation, exception handling, and real-time visibility The Profile We Need This role will suit someone who has built and scaled engineering in regulated or regulation-adjacent fintech and can combine speed with controls. data.title: Head of Change Management
data.jd_text: Job Summary The Change Management section is situated directly under the Digital Office Manager, reporting to the Chief Technical Officer. This section serves the crucial purpose of managing and facilitating organizational changes related to digital technologies, processes, and people, particularly in relation to the implementation of the Digital Strategy, Plans, and resulting Digital Initiatives at QAFCO. Change Management is considered a structured approach to transitioning individuals, teams, and QAFCO as a whole from the current state to a desired future state. In the context of a Digital Office, where technology and processes are rapidly evolving, effective Change Management is a critical function that plays a pivotal role in ensuring that technological changes are smoothly integrated into the organization, maximizing benefits while minimizing disruptions and resistance. Additionally, the role will leverage Artificial Intelligence (AI), predictive analytics, and data-driven insights to monitor adoption trends, predict resistance patterns, personalize engagement, and enhance the overall effectiveness and agility of QAFCO’s digital transformation initiatives. The Head of Change Management will act as a bridge between digital transformation and human adoption by utilizing AI-assisted tools for measuring readiness, adoption progress, and communication effectiveness. The role requires the ability to interpret AI-generated insights and translate them into tailored change interventions that improve user experience, compliance, and overall digital maturity within QAFCO’s evolving digital ecosystem. The Head of Change Management Section will have the overall responsibility for planning, facilitating, and ensuring the timely delivery and sustained adoption of the Change Management Programs, including digital initiatives and AI use cases, through a structured change process, stakeholder engagement, continuous improvement methodologies, and reinforcement mechanisms. Key Accountabilities Lead and Execute Comprehensive Change Management Strategies: Drive the execution of Change Management strategies, ensuring seamless transitions in technology, processes, and organizational structure within the Digital Office. Utilize AI-assisted tools and analytics to identify adoption patterns, stakeholder sentiment, and areas requiring targeted interventions. Minimize Resistance through Effective Communication and Training: Identify and address concerns to minimize resistance by implementing effective communication, training, and support initiatives. Ensure Alignment with Business Objectives and Foster Collaboration: Align digital initiatives with overall business objectives fostering collaboration between teams and various business units. Optimize Technology Adoption with Training Programs: Implement robust training programs and support mechanisms to optimize the adoption of new technologies among end-users driven from the change impact and learning needs assessments.

data.title: Director – Infrastructure Operations
data.jd_text: Established in 2004, Dubai Holding is a global investment company with investments in more than 34 countries and a combined workforce of nearly 45,000 individuals. In line with the vision of Dubai’s leadership and economic diversification strategy, Dubai Holding companies have nurtured sectors, irrevocably transforming Dubai’s socio-economic landscape and positioning Dubai as a diversified, globally integrated economy. Dubai Holding is committed to the diversification of Dubai’s non-oil economy. Our portfolio, valued at over AED 284+ billion, spans 10 sectors, including real estate, hospitality, leisure & entertainment, ICT, design, education, media, retail, manufacturing & logistics, and science. For the Good of Tomorrow Dubai Holding is looking to hire a Director - Infrastructure Operations reporting to the Vice President - Cloud, Infrastructure and Workplace Services. This is a senior leadership role accountable for the design, delivery, and continuous evolution of Dubai Holding's infrastructure services portfolio, spanning compute, storage, data centre, network, and Microsoft 365 environments. The role holds end-to-end ownership of infrastructure operations across the organisation, orchestrating the combined capabilities of internal teams, strategic third-party partners, and emerging technologies to deliver resilient, secure, and high-performing services at scale. A core contributor to the group-wide infrastructure strategy and technology roadmap, the Director plays an integral role in shaping and executing the direction of Dubai Holding's infrastructure function — ensuring all solutions and initiatives are grounded in sound architectural principles, aligned to security and audit requirements, and structured to deliver tangible business value. The Director is a change agent who thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. The role brings together strategic vision and operational rigour to drive technology modernisation, elevate service quality, enhance customer satisfaction, and achieve sustainable operational efficiency across the organisation. If you are ready to embark on a dynamic journey where your talents are valued, and your contributions make a real impact, we would like to hear from you. Key Responsibilities: Define and own the organisation's infrastructure architecture strategy, ensuring solutions are scalable, resilient, and secure. Partner with cross-functional teams and business verticals to design and deliver infrastructure that meets both current demands and future growth requirements. Take executive ownership of the availability, stability, recoverability, and performance of all infrastructure services across the organisation, ensuring they remain cost-optimised, fit for purpose, and aligned with agreed service levels. Candidates should have prior ECM experience covering MENA markets, though they do not need to be currently based in Riyadh [1] RESPONSIBILITIES: Lead and manage the execution of equity capital markets transactions across the MENA region, including IPOs, follow-on offerings, rights issues, block trades, and private placements Coordinate closely with internal teams including investment banking coverage, syndicate, research, legal, and compliance to ensure smooth transaction execution Oversee the preparation of marketing materials, investor presentations, prospectuses, and other transaction documentation Manage transaction timelines and due diligence processes from mandate through to pricing and closing Support senior bankers in originating ECM opportunities and preparing client pitches and strategic advisory materials Maintain relationships with corporate clients, financial sponsors, and government-related entities across the region Monitor equity capital markets activity across MENA, providing insights on market conditions, investor sentiment, and valuation trends Supervise and mentor Associates and Analysts, ensuring high-quality analytical work and presentation materials [2] REQUIREMENTS: 6–10 years of experience in Equity Capital Markets or Investment Banking Demonstrated experience executing equity transactions across MENA markets Experience at a leading international or regional investment bank Strong understanding of IPO processes, equity issuance, valuation, and capital markets structuring Strong financial modelling, analytical, and presentation skills Ability to manage multiple transactions in a fast-paced, deal-driven environment Strong interpersonal and client communication skills Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, or a related field Advanced qualifications such as an MBA or CFA are advantageous
